item.title: trust-mode.json and heartbeat.json written without mode 0600 — security config and health state world-readable
item.intent: trust-mode.json and heartbeat.json must be created with mode 0600 — trust-mode.json reveals the agent security posture and heartbeat.json reveals agent activity patterns. Both must be protected the same way credentials.json is.
item.description: src/cloud/trust-modes/switch.ts line 75: writeFileSync(tmpPath, content) has no mode option. trust-mode.json stores the agent trust mode (Paranoid/Zero-Trust/Managed) — revealing the security posture on a multi-user system allows an attacker to know whether the agent is configured to auto-approve cloud operations. src/cloud/heartbeat/reporter.ts line 71: writeFileSync(tmpPath, content) has no mode option. heartbeat.json stores health status and timing data. Both files follow the same write-to-tmp-then-rename pattern as commands.json (BUG-086) but without FILE_MODE_SECRET protection. Fix pattern is identical to BUG-086: add chmodSync import, add FILE_MODE_SECRET to existing defaults.js import, change writeFileSync to include { mode: FILE_MODE_SECRET }, and add chmodSync before renameSync. switch.ts has no existing defaults.js import — add one alongside the DIR_MODE_SECRET import added by BUG-085. reporter.ts already imports CLOUD_HEARTBEAT_RPC_TIMEOUT_MS from defaults.js — add FILE_MODE_SECRET there.
